title: eupatorium ranunculifolium
intro: Eupatorium ranunculifolium, also known as Butterweed, is a perennial plant native to the western United States and Canada, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and large, lobed leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of Eupatorium ranunculifolium include reducing pain and inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, which can be used topically to soothe skin irritations or internally to address various health concerns.
